subject: Arts

sections.heading: 🧙 The Wizard's Spell: What is Songwriting?
sections.body: Welcome, young hero, to the Quest of Songwriting! A song is like a magic spell — it uses words and melody to paint pictures in people's minds and touch their hearts. Songwriting is when YOU become the wizard who creates that spell. Think about how a Bollywood song makes you feel happy or sad — that is the power of a songwriter's words!
keyPoints: Songwriting is expressing your thoughts and feelings through words set to music
Every great song starts with an idea — just like every great quest starts with a goal

sections.heading: 🔮 The Realm of Description: Painting Pictures with Words
sections.body: Descriptive writing is your most powerful weapon in this quest! It means describing something so clearly that the listener can almost see, smell, and feel it. Imagine describing a mango — not just saying 'it is sweet' but saying 'it is golden yellow, dripping with juice, smelling like summer at your nani's house.' That is descriptive writing! Use all five senses to make your song come alive.
keyPoints: Use all your senses — sight, smell, touch, taste, sound — to describe things in your song
Share your writing with friends and welcome their feedback; that is how wizards grow stronger
Read other people's writing too — it gives you new powers and ideas

sections.heading: 🐉 Taming the Dragon: Writing Lyrics to a Melody
sections.body: Here is a fun secret — you do not always need to create a new tune! You can take a melody you already know and write your own words to it. Remember 'Twinkle Twinkle Little Star'? The same tune is used in 'Baa Baa Black Sheep' and the ABC song! In Indian music, there is something called Nottuswaram — a set of 39 songs by the great composer Muthuswami Dikshitar that mix Western tunes with Indian music. You can do the same magic with any tune you love, even a cricket chant!
keyPoints: You can write brand new lyrics for a melody you already know — like remixing a favourite game
Nottuswaram is a special set of Carnatic music compositions that use Western melodies with Indian words
Try writing your own words for both familiar tunes and new, unfamiliar melodies to build your skills

sections.heading: ⚡ The Rhyming Power: Making Words Dance Together
sections.body: Rhyming is like a secret power that makes songs easy to remember and fun to hear. When words at the end of lines sound similar — like 'cat, bat, mat' or 'hand, understand' — our ears enjoy it and we listen more carefully. Think about how you remember rhymes from Diwali songs or nursery rhymes so easily — that is the magic of rhyme! A rhyme scheme is the pattern of which lines rhyme — for example, if lines 1 and 3 rhyme and lines 2 and 4 rhyme, that pattern is called ABAB.
keyPoints: Rhyming words at the end of lines help listeners know when one line ends and another begins
A rhyme scheme like ABAB or AABB is the pattern of rhyming lines in a song or poem
Rhymes make songs stick in your memory — just like the Solar System Song helps you remember all the planets!

sections.heading: 🏆 The Master Quest: Creating Your Own Original Song
sections.body: Now comes the final level of your quest — creating a completely original song! This means writing your own words AND making up your own tune. If you love expressing how you feel — about your friends, your favourite festival, or even your pet — you can turn those feelings into a song. India has many genres of music like Hindustani classical, Carnatic, folk, devotional, and film music. Each one is a different realm to explore. You, young hero, have the power to create something the world has never heard before!
keyPoints: Creating original lyrics and setting them to your own tune is the highest level of songwriting mastery
India has many music genres — classical, folk, devotional, film — each is a different world to explore and learn from
Songwriting is collaboration, not competition — help and encourage your fellow heroes on their quest

summary: In this exciting quest, young heroes discovered the art of songwriting — a wizard's spell that uses words and melody to touch hearts and paint pictures. They learned that descriptive writing uses all five senses to bring ideas alive, and that lyrics can be written for both familiar tunes (like Twinkle Twinkle) and new melodies. They explored the power of rhyming and rhyme schemes like ABAB that make songs memorable and musical. They also discovered India's rich musical world — from Nottuswaram by Dikshitar to Hindustani, Carnatic, and folk genres. Finally, they learned that the greatest quest of all is creating your own original song to share your unique voice with the world!
